items.question: Anthropic's corporate form as a public-benefit corporation primarily means:
options: It cannot sell products or accept investment
It may pursue a stated public benefit alongside profit
It is identical to a US 501(c)(3) charity
It must open-source all model weights
items.answer: It may pursue a stated public benefit alongside profit
items.explanation: PBCs remain for-profit with an encoded benefit mission.

items.question: Which phrase summarizes Anthropic's target assistant behavior shorthand?
items.explanation: Common Anthropic framing for alignment-oriented assistant goals.

items.question: Anthropic's mission primarily couples capability with:
options: Safety, steerability, and broadly distributed benefit
items.answer: Safety, steerability, and broadly distributed benefit
items.explanation: Mission pairs useful models with safety research and governance—not perfection or anti-commercial absolutism.

items.question: When Claude refuses a policy-violating request, that behavior most directly reflects:
options: A random bug in the chat UI
Mission and safety policy encoded in training and product rules
The model having no training at all
Users having no Usage Policy obligations
items.answer: Mission and safety policy encoded in training and product rules
items.explanation: Refusals operationalize alignment goals and current Usage Policy—not arbitrary glitches.
